x.bio: Tabs Chocolate (Exited) || Now building https://t.co/kO22YD6KLx → we hunt & kill copycats & counterfeits | Book a call below 👇

tweets.text:
Thought scaling would feel like "we made it."

Instead it's:

> Ads breaking at 2am
> Supplier ghosting mid-order
> 3PL shipping the wrong SKU
> Customer support blowing up

There's no "making it" in ecom.

U just get better problems.

U can tell if a brand is ngmi in 30 seconds.

Open the site:

> Pop-up hits instantly
> No social proof
> Landing page looks like 2018 dropshipping
> Checkout feels sketchy

But they're running $5k/day in ads.

Professionally lighting money on fire.

3 creatives.
1 angle.
Same offer for 6 weeks.

Then u ask "why is performance down?"

Because ur feeding the algo crumbs.

Agentic Commerce on a real store:

Looks like @Shopify has added official llms.txt support. No more hacky workarounds. 
You can override it with a `llms.txt.liquid` file, similar to how robots.txt works now.

tweets.text:
Are there ANY saas companies who run a really strong performance marketing machine?  

Thinking multi-channel ads here.

Who does this really well?

What he said.

I see too many founders optimizing towards top line growth "SCALE BRO!!" instead of maximizing free cash into their pockets.

Over the last 8 years I've made almost as much money from my investments compounding than I did from my first 2 exits.

If the average person can use AI to get their work done in half the time. 

Do they use the rest to do more work?

Or

Do they just take more time for other things?

Outside of entrepreneurs, I think most people choose to take the free time.
